page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the N2 machine family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon core running at 2.8 GHz with Hyper-Threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU includes support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, AES cryptography, and Intel Virtualization Technology. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the n2-standard-2 is positioned at the lower end of the compute spectrum. In stress-ng testing, single-core performance is weak, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read and write speeds measured at 13.8 GB/sec and 11.79 GB/sec respectively. While single-threaded gzip compression achieves average tier results, more complex workloads like software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing score in the bottom 10% on Geekbench. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also relatively low.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-2 offers a low-cost entry point for dedicated vCPU resources without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. However, the trade-off is limited throughput and compute capacity. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, small-scale development environments, simple background tasks, or low-traffic application testing where high performance is not a primary requirement.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is an entry-level general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It provides dedicated CPU allocation with Hyper-Threading across a single physical core, alongside support for AVX-512 and AES instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place the n2-standard-2 in the lower performance tiers, with weak single-core and poor multi-core compute metrics, as well as limited memory bandwidth. However, it maintains average performance in single-threaded gzip compression. Given its hardware profile, this instance offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring dedicated but modest resources. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, and low-intensity background processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It is designed for lightweight workloads requiring dedicated entry-level compute resources.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A dedicated entry-level compute instance with balanced memory and processor resources for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM on a dedicated Intel Xeon processor
Supports AVX-512 and AES hardware-accelerated instruction sets
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suitable for lightweight web serving and development environments
Cost-effective option for low-intensity background tasks
